mission.title: kairos: :penguin: Add ubuntu arm rpi flavor
mission.description:
**What this PR does / why we need it**:
Ubuntu flavors which support raspberrypi

**Which issue(s) this PR fixes** *(optional, in `fixes #<issue number>(, fixes #<issue_number>, ...)` format, will close the issue(s) when PR gets merged)*:
Fixes #645

resolution.summary: Hey @c0ffee thanks! that's looking good at a first glance! I didn't had a deeper look on it yet, but will do as soon as possible - we need also to create the repository for the flavors as well before merging it
steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
codeSnippets:
:mega: We’re building smart automated test selection to slash your CI/CD build times. [Learn more](https://about.codecov.io/iterative-testing/?utm_medium=referral&utm_source=github&utm_content=comment&utm_campaign=pr+comments&utm_term=kairos-io)

Hey @c0ffee thanks! that's looking good at a first glance! I didn't had a deeper look on it yet, but will do as soon as possible - we need also to create the repository for the flavors as well before merging it
@mudler yeap the repos have to exist good point. so far it is booting on a rpi4, but would be good if someone with deeper knowledge of kairos can have a look :)

the main point is the size, because of the uncompressed firmwares the 2gb standard size for rpi img wont be enough, how should we handle this? simple increase the limit for all images?
> @mudler yeap the repos have to exist good point. so far it is booting on a rpi4, but would be good if someone with deeper knowledge of kairos can have a look :)
> 
> the main point is the size, because of the uncompressed firmwares the 2gb standard size for rpi img wont be enough, how should we handle this? simple increase the limit for all images?

Very nice!


Maybe we can compress those Fw files somehow? If that is the main difference maybe we can get away with it.
https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/linux-firmware/+bug/1942260

not much movement there, maybe all are waiting for the zstd support in the kernel, but nevertheless this wouldnt help us on the lts releases :)
I've created the quay repository for the images (core and k3s):

- kairos-ubuntu-arm-rpi
- core-ubuntu-arm-rpi
- core-ubuntu-20-lts-arm-rpi
- kairos-ubuntu-20-lts-arm-rpi
- core-ubuntu-22-lts-arm-rpi
- kairos-ubuntu-22-lts-arm-rpi
Just a note: this is still a "draft" PR, although I think it's probably ready for review now :)
> Just a note: this is still a "draft" PR, although I think it's probably ready for review now :)

Yes I have to build all 3 images again and try it again on hardware, this is why I didn't change the status yet :)
